CURRENT TRUTH (from CLAUDE.md — what the notes must reflect):
- PRIMARY executor/doer = each member's own Hermes profile, reached via `python3 tools/dispatch.py <agent-name> "<prompt>"` (full terminal, full tools).
- SUBSTITUTE (only when Hermes is unreachable) = MiniMax M3 via `python3 star-alliance-arsenal/minimax.py "<prompt>"` (the older `~/.config/minimax/mm.py` still works too; key at ~/.config/minimax/m3.key). So MiniMax is the FALLBACK, NOT the primary.
- THINKER = each member's Claude session model — Sonnet per member, Opus for the Strategist (NOT universally "Opus/me"). The brain plans → prompts the doer → reviews → re-prompts until it conforms.
- ENFORCEMENT today is via hooks: executor-enforce (Butler blocked from direct writes), dispatch-enforce (specialist subagents blocked from direct writes — must route through tools/dispatch.py → Hermes), delegation-gate (inline doer-grade bulk with no doer call is blocked). `summon.py` is now LEGACY — the primary path is dispatch.py → Hermes. The usage-log.jsonl ledger + Arsenal dashboard banner part is still accurate, keep it.

EDITS TO MAKE (preserve each file's frontmatter and overall structure; change only the now-false claims):

1) minimax-doer-setup.md
   - Reframe MiniMax from "the PRIMARY doer" to "the SUBSTITUTE doer — used only when the member's Hermes profile (via tools/dispatch.py) is unreachable." Update the description: frontmatter line too.
   - Update the role-split section: the thinker is the member's Claude brain (Sonnet per member, Opus for the Strategist), not universally "Opus (me)". Hermes profile via dispatch.py is the primary doer; MiniMax M3 is the fallback for text-only bulk.

3) MEMORY.md (the index)

Repo: /Users/atta/Documents/Claude/Projects/star-alliance (Star Alliance dashboard project).

TASK: Add a "Model Control Panel" to the dashboard at dashboard.html / dashboard.js / app.css, served by serve.cjs (a plain Node http server, port 4178, currently only has one route: GET /api/status — see serve.cjs for the pattern).

REQUIREMENTS:
1. UI: a new panel/section in the dashboard (alongside the existing Roster section — see `renderRoster`/`memberCard` in dashboard.js for the existing per-member card pattern and window._GUILD_MEMBERS for the member list shape). For EACH guild member, render one row with:
   - A "Brain" dropdown with exactly these options: Opus, Sonnet, Haiku
   - A "Doer" dropdown with exactly these options: kimi-k2.7, glm-5.2, minimax-payg, minimax-sub
   - A Save button per row (or one global Save button that saves all changed rows — your call, keep it simple)
   Style it consistently with the existing app.css design tokens (don't hardcode new hex colors — reuse existing CSS variables/classes already used elsewhere in app.css).

2. PERSISTENCE — single source of truth, no second config file:
   - Add a new top-level `memberOverrides` object to `star-alliance-arsenal/models.json`, e.g.:
     `"memberOverrides": { "the-developer": { "brain": "sonnet", "doer": "glm-5.2" }, ... }`
   - Add two new routes to `serve.cjs`:
     - `GET /api/model-override` → reads `star-alliance-arsenal/models.json`, returns the `memberOverrides` object as JSON (create the file's `memberOverrides` key as `{}` if it doesn't exist yet — don't break existing JSON structure or other keys in the file).
     - `POST /api/model-override` → accepts `{ memberId, brain, doer }` in the JSON body, reads `star-alliance-arsenal/models.json`, updates (or inserts) `memberOverrides[memberId] = { brain, doer }`, writes the file back with the same `JSON.stringify(obj, null, 2)` formatting style already used elsewhere in this repo's JSON files, and returns `{ ok: true }`. Validate `brain` is one of opus/sonnet/haiku and `doer` is one of kimi-k2.7/glm-5.2/minimax-payg/minimax-sub — reject (400) anything else. Do NOT touch any other key in models.json when writing.
   - On dashboard load, dashboard.js should fetch `GET /api/model-override` and pre-fill each row's dropdowns with the saved selection (default to nothing/blank if no override saved yet for that member).
   - On Save click, dashboard.js POSTs the row's selection to `/api/model-override` and shows a brief inline confirmation (e.g. a checkmark or "Saved" text near the button that fades after ~2s). Handle fetch errors with a visible inline error, don't fail silently.

3. Do NOT touch `guild-data.json`, `guild-data.js`, `skill-md.js`, or `workflow-md.js` — those are build.py outputs and out of scope for this task. Do NOT run build.py.

4. This repo follows a "Hermes-first" doctrine for doer-grade bulk work — since you ARE the Hermes-side executor for this dispatch, just do the file edits directly with your own tools.

5. After implementing, verify the JSON you write to models.json is valid (e.g. `python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('star-alliance-arsenal/models.json'))"`), and verify serve.cjs has no syntax errors (`node -c serve.cjs`).

Report back: which files you changed, the exact shape of the `memberOverrides` JSON, and the two new endpoint behaviors.

Plan (do NOT execute — planning only, read-only) an Architecture Build for the star-alliance repo. The Guild Master has locked a final model architecture and wants everything that disagrees purged, plus the dashboard turned into a REAL mission-control that actually sets the default models.

LOCKED DECISIONS (do not re-litigate these):
- RULE: Claude models = BRAIN (thinker). Non-Claude models = DOER. This is the dispatch.py architecture. Anything tagging a non-Claude model as role 'thinker'/'both', or defaulting a brain seat to a non-Claude model, is a contradiction to purge.
- Final canonical roster:
  • Brains (Claude, backend=claude): opus, sonnet, haiku
  • Doers (non-Claude): kimi-k2.7, glm-5.2, minimax-payg, minimax-sub
  • Media (non-Claude, role=media, KEEP — they power imagegen/voice/video/music tooling): image-01, minimax-video, minimax-speech, minimax-music
- PURGE from models.json entirely: deepseek-v4-pro, nemotron-3-ultra, qwen3.5, gemma4 (non-Claude thinker models not in the Guild Master's dashboard picture).
- RENAME/SPLIT: minimax-m3 is replaced by TWO real billing variants: minimax-payg (pay-as-you-go API key) and minimax-sub (subscription key). Both are doers. Every reference to minimax-m3 across the repo must migrate to the new scheme.

GROUND TRUTH I already confirmed this session (build on it, verify if unsure):
- models.json (star-alliance-arsenal/) is the single source of truth for the model roster. It has a `seats` block (brain/doer/critic/bench defaults+fallbacks), a `models` dict, a `swarm` block, and an empty `memberOverrides` block.
- Current WRONG state in models.json: seats.brain.default = 'minimax-m3' (non-Claude — violates the rule); 6 non-Claude models tagged role 'thinker' (deepseek-v4-pro, glm-5.2, kimi-k2.7, nemotron-3-ultra, qwen3.5, gemma4); seat fallback chains reference soon-purged models (deepseek-v4-pro etc.); swarm.comment says brain='GLM-5.2'. There are _note fields about a 'temporary' Ollama 429 outage that are now moot under the new architecture.
- THE DASHBOARD IS A FAKE CONTROL PANEL: dashboard.js hardcodes BRAIN_OPTIONS (opus/sonnet/haiku) and DOER_OPTIONS (kimi-k2.7/glm-5.2/minimax-payg/minimax-sub) — NOT derived from models.json. serve.cjs hardcodes VALID_BRAINS/VALID_DOERS (same lists) and its POST /api/model-override handler writes the pick into models.json → memberOverrides[memberId] = {brain, doer}. BUT nothing reads memberOverrides except serve.cjs itself — dispatch.py does NOT read it. So the Guild Master's dashboard picks currently change NOTHING about real routing. minimax-payg/minimax-sub appear ONLY in those two hardcoded UI lists — they are not real registry entries yet.
- dispatch.py: AGENTS dict maps agent-name → Hermes profile name; calls `hermes -p <profile> --yolo chat -q <prompt>`. The doer model is currently chosen Hermes-side (in ~/.hermes/profiles/<agent>/config.yaml, which is published from repo profiles/<agent>/config.yaml via tools/publish_profiles.py). So making the dashboard control the ACTUAL default doer means the dashboard's saved choice must propagate to whatever dispatch/execution actually reads (either models.json seats/memberOverrides that dispatch.py is taught to read, or the Hermes profile configs). Design this propagation path — this is the crux of 'real mission control'.
- SECURITY-SENSITIVE: .claude/hooks/executor-enforce.py mechanically pins the string 'minimax-m3' (Task/Agent spawns must pin model=minimax-m3 to delegate the executor seat). CLAUDE.md documents this. Renaming minimax-m3 → payg/sub touches this enforcement hook and MUST be handled carefully or the executor lock breaks. Flag this as a high-risk item.
- CLAUDE.md has a seat doctrine table (currently 'Thinker=GLM-5.2, Doer=MiniMax M3, Critic=Kimi K2.7') that contradicts the new rule and must be rewritten to Claude=brain / non-Claude=doer.
- conformity_check.py has an ST check (seats valid + critic-family != brain-family) and BR/FB checks. Under the new rule it should mechanically ENFORCE: brain models must be backend=claude; doer seats must be non-Claude. Propose the check change so this never drifts again.
- Generated/derived that must be regenerated not hand-edited: star-alliance-arsenal/models/*.md (per-model docs via gen_model_docs.py), guild-data.js/json (build.py), VERSIONS.md. minimax.py defaults to model 'minimax-m3' and reads ~/.config/minimax/m3.key — the payg/sub split may need two keys or a key-selection arg; investigate and flag.
- The CRITIC seat: under the strict two-role member model (dashboard shows only brain+doer per member), decide what happens to the separate 'critic' seat concept in models.json + verify-gate.py. The verify-gate critic currently runs glm-5.2. Propose: does critic stay as a non-Claude reviewer (glm-5.2 is a valid non-Claude doer-family model, fine to keep as the critic), or fold away? Recommend, don't decide unilaterally — flag for Guild Master.

YOUR DELIVERABLE — a tight, plain-English, PHASED plan a non-programmer Guild Master can approve, covering:
1. Every file that must change, grouped by phase, with a one-line purpose each.
2. The design for how the dashboard SAVE becomes REAL control (the propagation path from dashboard pick → models.json → dispatch.py/Hermes actually honoring it). This is the centerpiece — be concrete.
3. How the dashboard dropdowns stop being hardcoded and derive from models.json (kill the triple-SoT: dashboard.js + serve.cjs + registry).
4. The minimax-m3 → payg/sub migration, explicitly calling out the executor-enforce.py security hook and how to migrate it without breaking the executor lock.
5. The conformity_check.py rule change that mechanically enforces Claude=brain/non-Claude=doer going forward.
6. An explicit DESTRUCTIVE-ACTIONS list (every model deletion, every file/entry removal, the hook edit) that needs the Guild Master's confirm-before-execute.
7. Flagged risks + the 1-2 open recommendations (esp. the critic seat) that still need a Guild Master yes/no.
8. Recommended execution order and which phases are safe-auto vs must-confirm.

Keep it structured and concise — this gets relayed by the Butler to a non-technical Guild Master. No raw code dumps; name files by what they do.

You are the Strategist. Plan (do NOT execute — return a wave plan) an Architecture Build for the Star Alliance mission-control dashboard. The Guild Master's approved goal, verbatim intent:

"The entire mission control (the Star Alliance dashboard) must READ all its data via scripts from each source of truth — NOTHING hardcoded except the artwork. Every domain/project must carry a version, visible on its Domains card, and the Guild Master wants to follow how each advanced over time."

GROUND TRUTH already established (do not re-investigate these — they are confirmed):
- Mission control = the Star Alliance dashboard (dashboard.html / dashboard.js), Domains section rendered by renderDomains() at dashboard.js:336. It reads GUILD data from guild-data.js/json, generated by build.py.
- data/domains.json holds the domains (projects), each with a `path`. Version 1, 3 domains, currently 0 have a version field.
- Three domains and their TRUE version sources:
  • Star Alliance → already script-derived from its guild-log replay as SemVer (build.py lines ~84-146, read_version). Current 6.63.105. Already correct — do not change how it's computed.
  • Lex Council App → /Users/attaselim/Documents/Claude/Projects/Lex Council App/lex_council/apps/web/package.json  → version 1.7.108 (also git tags v1.7.x). Read-only: the script reads this file, never writes it.
  • Lex Council Business → /Users/attaselim/Documents/Claude/Projects/Lex Council Business/  → NO version yet. Approved decision: version is AUTO-DERIVED from its vault-logs (same spirit as Star Alliance's log-replay), NOT a hand-bumped number. It has a vault-logs area (The Business/vault-logs). You must design how the version is derived from those logs.

APPROVED SCOPE (option b): 
1. A single version-reader script (Python, lives in star-alliance, e.g. tools/) that reads each domain's real version from its own source and writes them into the domains data that build.py feeds to the dashboard — never hardcoded. For Lex Council Business, derive the version from its vault-logs.
2. Create the version-derivation source/logic for Lex Council Business from its vault-logs.
3. Show each version on its Domains card in the dashboard (the existing card-tooltip system at dashboard.js ~242/279/336 already exists; add version there / a small badge).
4. Audit dashboard.js + guild-data generation for ANY remaining hardcoded data and confirm all of it is script-sourced — art is the ONLY allowed fixed thing. Report anything hardcoded that must be moved to a source.
5. "Follow how it advanced" — the Guild Master wants version HISTORY, not just current number. Design the lightest single-source mechanism (e.g. an append-only version ledger the reader emits, or per-domain history the dashboard can show on the tooltip). Keep it single-source.

Constraints/doctrine:
- Single source of truth: never hand-edit a generated file. Versions live in their true source; the dashboard shows a generated view.
- The version-reader must read the two external Lex projects READ-ONLY.
- Respect build.py as the generator; wire the reader into the build so guild-data.json carries the versions.
- The Developer (sonnet thinker + Hermes doer) will execute. Offload bulk to the Hermes doer.

Return: (a) the workflow name (Architecture Build), (b) an ordered wave plan with each wave's concrete deliverable and which files it touches, (c) your design for the Lex Council Business vault-log version derivation, (d) your design for the version-history mechanism, (e) any risks/open questions. Be concrete and file-specific. Do not write any files.

tool_input.prompt:
Workflow: Architecture Build. Guild Master approved. The Strategist already routed this and confirmed the plan; the-architect's Hermes profile is stalling, so you are the fallback doer seat. Anchors are pre-confirmed: mirror the FB check at conformity_check.py line ~623 and the RG check at line ~686.

TASK: Edit ONLY tools/conformity_check.py to add two new mechanical drift-guard checks, so duplicated facts cannot silently drift:

1. MODEL-DRIFT (call it "MD"): scan CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and every hook under .claude/hooks/*.py and .claude/hooks/*.sh for hardcoded model IDs. The authoritative model-ID set is the keys under the model registry in star-alliance-arsenal/models.json (the same registry the FB check already loads). Any model ID token referenced in those doctrine/hook files that is NOT present in models.json is a hard failure — this catches stale/renamed model names. Match model IDs conservatively (e.g. the known registry IDs like minimax-sub, minimax-payg, glm-5.2, kimi-k2.7, opus, sonnet, haiku and any others actually in models.json) — detect occurrences of a registry-shaped token and verify it exists in the registry; do not flag ordinary English words. Reuse the registry-loading helper the FB check already uses. Emit failures in the same "MD <file>: ..." style as existing checks and add MD to the summary/roster line.

2. ROSTER-DRIFT (call it "MR"): two hooks hardcode the member roster as a Python list literal — .claude/hooks/routing-enforce.py and .claude/hooks/hermes-upgrade-notify.py (look for a list of "the-architect", "the-developer", ... strings). Assert each hardcoded roster equals the true member roster derived from the star-alliance-members/*.md filenames (stem = agent id). Any mismatch (missing or extra member) = hard failure, emitted as "MR <file>: ...". Add MR to the summary/roster line.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Edit ONLY tools/conformity_check.py. Do NOT touch AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, the hooks, or models.json. AGENTS.md is a distinct Hermes-runtime doc, not a copy of CLAUDE.md — do not mirror them.
- Follow the existing check structure/idioms in the file (how FB and RG append to `fails`, load the registry, print). Keep it clean and consistent.
- The two new checks must run and report; it is EXPECTED that MD flags the ~19 hooks that hardcode glm-5.2 IF that model id is genuinely absent from models.json — but glm-5.2 IS in models.json, so those references are VALID and must NOT be flagged. MD only fires on IDs missing from the registry (true drift). Confirm this logic: a hardcoded glm-5.2 that exists in models.json passes; a hardcoded "gpt-5.5" or a renamed-away id fails.

AFTER EDITING: run `python3 tools/conformity_check.py` and report (a) that it parses/runs, (b) exactly what MD and MR report, (c) whether any real drift was surfaced. Do not try to fix any drift the new checks find — surfacing it is the deliverable. Report the final state plainly.

tool_input.prompt:
APPROVED Architecture Build — STEP 4 in repo /Users/attaselim/Documents/Claude/Projects/star-alliance. Guild Master said GO. You OWN this step. Child session: route file writes through `python3 tools/dispatch.py the-developer "<instruction>"` (verified working via minimax-payg). Read-only Bash/Read/Grep allowed directly.

GOAL: let the deployment brief + banner-enforcer express a swarm as an instance count ("The Architect ×7") instead of a single bullet — ADDITIVE, so non-swarm turns render byte-identically to today.

EXACT WORK-ORDER:
1. READ `.claude/hooks/workflow-banner-enforcer.py` fully — focus on the deployment-brief template block (~lines 237–238, `Deploying <N> agents:` + member bullets) and the member-detection regex (`MEMBER_RES` ~line 268). Understand how it currently validates the brief's agent bullets.
2. EDIT `.claude/hooks/workflow-banner-enforcer.py` — make the brief guidance additive: a swarm bullet may read `The <Member> ×<N> — <model>` (the `×N` / `xN` instance-count form documented in the decompose-and-swarm skill's "Deployment brief format"). CONFIRM the `MEMBER_RES` regex already tolerates a trailing `×N`/`xN` suffix (the skill claims it matches `The Developer x3 —`); if it does NOT, widen the regex minimally so `The Architect ×7 —` and `The Architect x7 —` both parse as a valid member bullet. A brief with NO swarm bullet must still validate exactly as today.
3. EDIT `.claude/hooks/guild-routing-gate.sh` — mirror the same instance-count guidance in its doctrine/brief-format prose block (it is the prose mirror of the enforcer; keep wording consistent).

CONSTRAINT: additive only. Do not change how non-swarm briefs are validated. This step has NO conformity check of its own (it's hook behavior).

VERIFY (manual, since no sweep covers it):
- Sanity-run the enforcer or the gate against a test brief containing `The Architect ×7 — opus` and confirm it does NOT reject it. Use a read-only invocation, e.g. pipe a small JSON stub through the hook the way the harness does, or run the file's __main__/self-test if present. Show the actual command + output.
- Confirm a plain single-bullet brief still passes.
- Run `python3 tools/conformity_check.py` only to confirm you introduced ZERO new failures (baseline is 13).

Return: exact edits to both files (show the regex before/after if you changed it), the manual verification command + output proving `×7` parses and a plain brief still passes, and the conformity delta. Flag any deviation. If the regex already tolerated `×N`, say so explicitly and note no regex change was needed.

APPROVED Architecture Build — STEP 4, repo /Users/attaselim/Documents/Claude/Projects/star-alliance. Small, surgical, judgment edit to two hook files. TARGETS:

The decompose-and-swarm skill says the regex already matches `The Developer x3 —`. CONFIRM whether `MEMBER_RES` already tolerates a trailing `×N` / `xN`. If yes: change nothing in the regex, just add one guidance line to the template that a swarm bullet may read `The <Member> ×<N> — <model>`. tool_input.description: Step 6: wire swarm driver + SD check
tool_input.prompt:
APPROVED Architecture Build — STEP 6 (final, riskiest), repo /Users/attaselim/Documents/Claude/Projects/star-alliance. Guild Master said GO. You are the brain: design the contract, then route each write through `python3 tools/dispatch.py the-developer "<instruction>"` (works via minimax-payg). Read/Grep yours directly. The verify-gate critic will review your diff — keep it minimal and correct.

GROUND TRUTH (already verified — do not re-litigate): `guild/run.py` is a headless subprocess. It resolves each step to a script, a single `delegate()` doer call, or a human halt (see its loop ~line 208 and `resolve_step`). It has NO handling for `exec:spawn` / `parallel` / `swarm{}`. It **cannot** emit a harness Task, so it can never truly spawn parallel Claude members — that power belongs ONLY to the live session (the Butler/Strategist). Your job is to make run.py *recognize and record* a swarm step and *safely degrade*, NOT to fake parallel spawning.

DELIVER THREE THINGS (all minimal):

1. NEW `guild/swarm.py` — one small pure function `plan_swarm(step) -> dict`:
   - reads `step.get("swarm")`; loads caps from `data/harness.json` -> `swarm_caps` (`max_swarm`, `max_total_workers`), FAIL-SOFT fallback 5/12 (mirror how tools/conformity_check.py already loads them — single source, do not re-hardcode a different value).
   - decides worthiness from the fields present (has swarm dict, `max_instances` >= `min_instances` >= 2, a `partition`). Returns e.g. `{"fan": bool, "instances": <clamped to max_swarm>, "member": ..., "partition": ..., "reason": ...}`.
   - clamp instances to `max_swarm`; never exceed it.

2. EDIT `guild/run.py`:
   - Add a module-docstring contract line: run.py is **delegate-not-spawn** — it runs scripts, single `delegate()` doer calls, and human halts; parallel member fan-out (`exec:spawn`/`swarm`) is owned by the LIVE session via the harness Task tool. run.py records the swarm plan and degrades to a single `delegate()`.
   - In the step loop, BEFORE normal resolution, detect `step.get("exec") == "spawn" or step.get("swarm")`. If so: call `plan_swarm(step)`, print/log a clear `SWARM POINT: fan {instances}× {member} by {partition} — live-session driver required; headless run degrades to single worker` line into the run summary, then fall through to the EXISTING single-delegate resolution so a headless run still completes. Additive: steps without swarm/exec behave exactly as today.

3. EDIT `tools/conformity_check.py` — add check `SD` (Swarm Declared-consumed): assert every workflow step that declares `swarm{}` (or `exec:spawn`) is actually consumed by run.py — mechanically, that `guild/run.py` contains the swarm-detection branch (grep its source for the `swarm`/`exec == "spawn"` handling) AND every swarm-declaring step also sets `exec:"spawn"`. On violation append a clear `SD ...` failure. Register in house style like the SC/SW checks.

VERIFY: `python3 build.py` clean; `python3 tools/conformity_check.py` — SW1–SW5 + your new SD all green, and ZERO new failures beyond the known 13 baseline. Then a real proof: `python3 guild/run.py "Strategic Audit" --dry-run` (or the nearest invocation) and show that the Convene-the-Panel step now prints a `SWARM POINT:` line. If run.py's CLI differs, adapt and show the actual command.

Return: the new file + both diffs (minimal), the SD registration, `conformity_check.py` result (SW+SD green, zero new), and the dry-run output proving the SWARM POINT line. Flag any deviation or anything the critic should scrutinize.

APPROVED Architecture Build — STEP 6 (final), repo /Users/attaselim/Documents/Claude/Projects/star-alliance. You are the brain: decide the contract, then route each small write through `python3 tools/dispatch.py the-developer "<instruction>"` (works via minimax-payg). Read/Grep yours. The verify-gate critic reviews your diff — keep it tight and correct.

GROUND TRUTH (verified, do not re-check): `guild/run.py` is a headless subprocess. Its step loop (~line 208) resolves each step to a script, a single `delegate()` call, or a human halt. It has no notion of `exec:spawn` / `parallel` / `swarm`. It cannot call the harness Task tool, so it can never truly spawn parallel members — only the live session can. Your goal: run.py should RECOGNISE a swarm step, record the plan, and safely degrade to one worker. Do NOT fake parallelism.

Three small pieces:

1. A new file `guild/swarm.py` with one pure function `plan_swarm(step) -> dict`. It reads `step.get("swarm")`, loads the caps from `data/harness.json` key `swarm_caps` (`max_swarm`, `max_total_workers`) fail-soft to 5/12 the same way `tools/conformity_check.py` already reads them (one source of truth — reuse, don't re-hardcode a value). It judges worthiness from the fields (swarm present, `max_instances >= min_instances >= 2`, a `partition`) and returns `{fan, instances, member, partition, reason}` with `instances` clamped to `max_swarm`.

2. A small change to `guild/run.py`: (a) one docstring line stating run.py is delegate-not-spawn — it runs scripts, single `delegate()` calls, human halts; parallel member fan-out (`exec:spawn`/`swarm`) belongs to the live session, and run.py records the plan then degrades to a single worker. (b) In the step loop, before normal resolution, when `step.get("exec") == "spawn" or step.get("swarm")`, call `plan_swarm`, write a `SWARM POINT: fan {instances}× {member} by {partition} — live-session driver required; headless run degrades to single worker` line into the run summary, then continue into the existing single-`delegate()` path. A step without swarm behaves exactly as before.

3. A small addition to `tools/conformity_check.py`: a check `SD` (swarm declared-consumed) asserting every workflow step that declares `swarm` (or `exec:spawn`) is consumed — i.e. `guild/run.py` source contains the swarm-detection branch, AND every swarm-declaring step also sets `exec:"spawn"`. Append a clear `SD ...` line on any violation. Register it in the same style as the existing SC / SW checks.

Proof to run and report: `python3 build.py` clean; `python3 tools/conformity_check.py` with SW1–SW5 and SD all green and no new failures past the known 13; then `python3 guild/run.py "Strategic Audit" --dry-run` showing the panel step prints a `SWARM POINT:` line (adapt the command if the CLI differs, show what you ran).

Report: the new file, both diffs, the SD registration, the conformity result, and the dry-run proof. Flag anything the critic should scrutinise.
